description.content: Tests for the POST /auth endpoint that issues access tokens.
Generates the auth token stored in the environment for use by all authenticated requests.
Obtains a valid auth token using demo credentials. Stores the token as an environment variable.
Verifies that valid credentials return a well-formed token with the correct response shape.
Expects status 200, a non-empty token string, correct Content-Type, and response time under 500ms.
Verifies the API rejects invalid credentials with the expected error response.
Sends a valid username with an incorrect password. Expects the API to return a 'Bad credentials' reason.
Sends a non-existent username. Expects the API to return a 'Bad credentials' reason.
Sends a request with no body. Expects the API to reject it without issuing a token.
Verifies the API does not execute injected payloads passed as credential values.
Sends a SQL injection string as the username. Expects no server error and no token in the response.

exec: pm.test("SQL Injection did not return a token", () => {

description.content: Sends an XSS payload as the username. Expects no server error and no token in the response.

exec: pm.test("XSS attempt did not return a token", () => {

description.content: Spot-checks API response time to catch latency regressions.
Asserts GET /booking responds in under 500ms as a baseline performance check.
Tests for the /booking endpoint covering CRUD operations on booking resources.
Creates the baseline booking used by all happy path and negative tests in this module.
Creates a booking for Brandon Sanderson and stores the booking_id and all field values as collection variables for downstream tests.

pm.test("Response contains booking with all sent fields", function () {

description.content: Verifies all supported read and write operations against a valid booking.
Expects a non-empty array of booking IDs from GET /booking.
Fetches the booking created in Setup and validates the full response schema and all field values.

exec: pm.test("Booking data matches setup values", function () {

description.content: Filters bookings by firstname and lastname. Expects the Setup booking to appear in the results.

pm.test("Created booking id exists in response", function () {

description.content: Filters bookings by checkin and checkout query params. Verifies the endpoint accepts date filters.

exec: // Known limitation: Restful-Booker's date filter is unreliable —
// it does not consistently return bookings whose checkin/checkout
// match the filter values. We verify the endpoint responds correctly
// and returns the expected shape, but skip the ID inclusion check.

description.content: Replaces the entire booking record via PUT. Verifies all updated fields are persisted correctly.
Updates only firstname via PATCH. Verifies the change is applied without affecting other fields.
Deletes the Setup booking. Expects 201 as the API's delete confirmation status.
Fetches the deleted booking. Expects 404 to confirm the record no longer exists.

exec: pm.test("Status code is 404 - booking no longer exists", function () {

description.content: Verifies the API returns appropriate errors for invalid inputs and unauthorized requests.
Requests a booking with a non-existent numeric ID. Expects 404.
Submits a booking without the required bookingdates field. Expects a 4xx error response.

exec: pm.test("Status code is 500 - missing required fields", function () {

description.content: Attempts a full update without providing an auth token. Expects 403 Forbidden.
Attempts to delete a booking without an auth token. Expects 403 Forbidden.
Tests boundary values and unusual inputs to verify the API handles them gracefully.
Verifies the API accepts 0 as a valid totalprice. Stores the booking ID for cleanup.

item.name: Create booking - special characters in name

description.content: Verifies the API accepts Unicode and special characters (José García-López) in name fields. Stores the booking ID for cleanup.
Sends totalprice as a string instead of a number. Documents the API's current permissive behavior.

exec: // Restful-Booker does not validate field types: sending totalprice as a string
// returns 200 and the API coerces or ignores the value.
// A stricter API should return 400 Bad Request.
pm.test("API returns 200 - observed: no type validation on totalprice", function () {

item.name: Update non-existent booking (API returns 405 - semantically should be 404)

description.content: Attempts to update booking ID 99999999. Documents the known API behavior: returns 405 instead of the semantically correct 404.

exec: // Restful-Booker returns 405 (Method Not Allowed) for non-existent booking IDs.
// The semantically correct response would be 404 (Not Found).
// This test documents the actual API behavior.
pm.test("Status code is 405 - observed API behavior for non-existent ID", function () {

description.content: Deletes the totalprice=0 booking to avoid polluting the dataset after the edge case run.
